key_insight: The paper turns numeric tables into searchable statistical embeddings, enabling similarity search and cross-dataset alignment without requiring matching feature names.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to retrieve related materials datasets, support model initialization, and choose algorithms for unfamiliar tabular data while preserving statistical context.
method: Extract structured exploratory-data-analysis descriptors from each numeric dataset.
Embed the descriptors into a shared vector space with a pretrained sentence transformer.
Use CCA, including a sparse penalized variant, to measure similarity and recover interpretable variable-level correspondences; optional differential privacy can be applied before embedding.
evidence_signal: Across 15 datasets, the approach achieved a total P@1 of 0.9, with nearest-neighbor retrieval and cluster structure remaining robust under embedding ablations and differential-privacy budgets.
watch_item: Interpretability and retrieval depend on the quality and consistency of the chosen descriptors, so sparse CCA alignments may still miss relationships not captured by the exploratory statistics.
paper_title: Statistical Embeddings for Similarity, Retrieval, and Interpretable Alignment of Numeric Tabular Datasets
